steps.title: Identify what must be the subject of 'cannot stand'
steps.content: The key signal is '[This hypothesis ____] cannot stand, researchers ... counter.' The researchers are arguing that the hypothesis itself is problematic or invalid, which means the hypothesis must be positioned as the subject of the predicate 'cannot stand.'

steps.title: Choose the form that establishes the hypothesis as subject
steps.content:
The participle 'suggesting' functions as a reduced relative clause modifier: “This hypothesis suggesting that trees survived cannot stand” makes the entire noun phrase the subject of 'cannot stand,' clearly establishing that the hypothesis is invalid unless other explanations are ruled out.

A finite verb like 'suggests' would make the that-clause its direct object, leaving ambiguous what cannot stand; 'suggested' has the same structural problem with mismatched tense; 'has suggested' doesn't fit the context of recent analyses.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
